elements.sent-2411-006: The focus of this article is the intersection of three topics in random matrix theory: addition of random matrices, $\beta$-ensembles, and edge universality.
elements.sent-2411-007: The study of addition of independent large random matrices dates back to Voiculescu, in which the author considers \begin{equation}C(N)=A(N)+B(N),\end{equation} where $A(N)$, $B(N)$ are two independent $N\times N$ real/complex self-adjoint matrices with deterministic eigenvalues $a_{1 (N)\ge ...\ge a_{N (N)$, $b_{1 (N)\ge...\ge b_{N (N)$ and uniformly random eigenvectors.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-008: Voiculescu \cite{Vo proves that if the empirical measures of $A(N)$, $B(N)$ have \begin{equation}\frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N}\delta_{a_{i}(N)}\rightarrow \mu_{A},\quad \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N}\delta_{b_{i}(N)}\rightarrow \mu_{B}\end{equation} for some deterministic measures $\mu_{A}$, $\mu_{B}$ on $\R$, then \begin{equation}\frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N}\delta_{c_{i}(N)}\rightarrow \mu_{C},\end{equation} where \begin{equation}\mu_{C}=\mu_{A}\boxplus\mu_{B}\end{equation} is also a deterministic measure on $\R$, known as the free convolution of $\mu_{A}$ and $\mu_{B}$.}
elements.sent-2411-009: This classical result was an early breakthrough in the subject called free probability, that has various interplay with different types of random matrix additions, see e.g \cite{B1 , \cite{B2 , \cite{MSS .}}}

elements.sent-2411-152: We will focus on two particular matrix ensembles: the Gaussian $\beta$ ensembles and the Laguerre $\beta$ ensembles.
elements.sent-2411-153: For $\beta>0$, a $N\times N$ Gaussian $\beta$-ensemble is a random N-tuples $(\lambda_{1}\ge ...\ge \lambda_{N )\in \R^{N}$ with density function \begin{equation}\label{eq_Gaussiandensity} \frac{1}{Z_{N,\beta}}\prod_{1\le i<j\le N}(\lambda_{i}-\lambda_{j})^{\beta}\prod_{i=1}^{N}\exp\left(-\frac{\lambda_{i}^{2}}{2N}\right),\end{equation} and a $N\times L$ Laguerre $\beta$-ensemble ($N\le L)$ is a random N-tuples $(\lambda_{1}\ge ...\ge \lambda_{N )\in \R^{N}$ with density function \begin{equation}\label{eq_Laguerre} \frac{1}{Z_{N,L,\beta}}\prod_{i=1}^{N}\Big[\lambda_{i}^{\frac{\beta}{2}(L-N+1)-1}\exp\left(-\frac{\beta\lambda_{i}}{2} \right)\Big]\prod_{1\le i\le j\le N}(\lambda_{i}-\lambda_{j})^{\beta}.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-154: For the $N\times N$ G$\beta$E the Bessel generating function is given by \begin{equation}G_N(z_1,\ldots,z_N;\theta)= \exp\left(\frac{N(z_1^2+\cdots +z_N^2)}{2\theta}\right).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-155: For the $N\times L$ L$\beta$E the Bessel generating function is given by \begin{equation}G_N(z_1,\ldots,z_N;\theta)=\prod_{i=1}^{N}\frac{\exp(-Lz_{i})}{\left(1-\frac{z_{i}}{\theta}\right)^{L\theta}}.\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-995: Take $z=z_c+\epsilon e^{i\theta}$. \begin{equation} \frac{1}{2\pi i} \int_{C_{\epsilon}} \; \frac{1}{(1-z/z_c)^2} V(z)^{L+1} \; dz = \; \frac{1}{2\pi} \int_{3\pi/2}^{\pi/2} \; \frac{z_c^2}{\epsilon} e^{-i\theta} V(z_c+\epsilon e^{i\theta})^{L+1} \; d\theta \approx \; \frac{1}{2\pi} \int_{3\pi/2}^{\pi/2} \; \frac{z_c^2}{\epsilon} e^{-i\theta} V(z_c)^{L+1} \; d\theta = \; \frac{1}{\epsilon}\frac{z_c^2 V(z_c)^{L+1}}{\pi} \end{equation} The second line follows from Taylor expanding $V(z_c+\epsilon e^{i\theta )^{L+1}$ around its critical point $z_c$ with $\epsilon = \frac{1 {L}$, \begin{equation} V(z_c+\epsilon e^{i\theta})^{L+1} = \; V(z_c)^{L+1} + \frac{1}{2}(L+1)V(z_c)^L V''(z_c)\epsilon^2 e^{2i\theta} + O\left(L^2\epsilon^3\right) = \;V(z_c)^{L+1} + \frac{1}{2}\frac{L+1}{L^2}V(z_c)^L V''(z_c) e^{2i\theta} + O\left(\frac{1}{L}\right) \approx \; V(z_c)^{L+1} \end{equation} Looking only at the two terms that are proportional to $\frac{1 {\epsilon}$, we see that \begin{equation} - \frac{1}{\epsilon\sqrt{L+1}} \frac{\sqrt{L+1}z_c^2V(z_c)^{L+1}}{\pi} e^{-\frac{f''(z_c)}{2}\epsilon^2(L+1)} + \frac{1}{\epsilon}\frac{z_c^2 V(z_c)^{L+1}}{\pi} = \; - \frac{1}{\epsilon} \frac{z_c^2V(z_c)^{L+1}}{\pi} e^{-\frac{f''(z_c)}{2}\epsilon^2(L+1)} + \frac{1}{\epsilon}\frac{z_c^2 V(z_c)^{L+1}}{\pi} \approx \; - \frac{1}{\epsilon} \frac{z_c^2V(z_c)^{L+1}}{\pi} \left(1 -\frac{f''(z_c)}{2}\epsilon^2(L+1)\right) + \frac{1}{\epsilon}\frac{z_c^2 V(z_c)^{L+1}}{\pi} = \; \frac{z_c^2V(z_c)^{L+1}}{\pi} \frac{f''(z_c)}{2}\epsilon(L+1). \end{equation} Note that with $\epsilon=\frac{1 {L}$ this is $O\left(V(z_c)^{L+1}\right)$ and will be small compared to the remaining term which we compute below.}}}}
